summary: Frontend-only validation completed on public preview URL for hero image sizing follow-up. Desktop height parity is correct, but requested removal of overlay/card-like image treatment is not fully implemented.

ui_bugs.issue: Hero image frame still uses card-like container styling (rounded corners, border, shadow), which conflicts with request to remove extra padding/card spacing feel.

issues: Gradient overlay layer remains configured on .hero-frame::before (linear-gradient), conflicting with no-overlay requirement for pure image area.
Image panel still appears as a stylized card rather than a plain full-column visual block.

action_items: Remove .hero-frame::before gradient overlay and related pseudo-element layering.
Remove/flatten card-like frame styles on .hero-frame (border, shadow, heavy radius) to match requested full-column image treatment.
Re-verify desktop hero panel after style updates to ensure full-height match remains intact.
critical_code_review_comments: App.css still defines explicit decorative overlay for hero image via .hero-frame::before despite requirement for no overlays in hero image area.
Hero frame visual treatment (border/shadow/radius) introduces a boxed card impression, likely causing repeated sizing/spacing feedback from user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified on public URL from frontend/.env. Desktop hero copy panel and image frame heights match exactly (diff 0.00). No text is rendered inside image frame. Remaining gap is visual styling mismatch with user request: card-like frame + configured pseudo-overlay gradient.
rca of the issue: RCA: requirement mismatch is CSS-level, not rendering engine issue. Reproduction: open landing page desktop and inspect .hero-frame in App.css; observed border-radius, border, box-shadow, and ::before gradient declaration. These styles create non-flat panel treatment and perceived overlay/card spacing despite correct height alignment.
